steps.title: Recognize the no-solution condition
steps.content: A system of two linear equations has no solution exactly when the two lines are parallel — they have the same slope but different y-intercepts, so they never intersect.

steps.title: Find the slope of the given equation
steps.content:
The given equation can be written as $8x - 31ry = -c$ (a constant on the right). Solve for $y$ to put it in slope-intercept form:
$$-31ry = -8x - c$$$$y = \frac{8}{31r}\,x + \frac{c}{31r}$$The slope of the given line is $\frac{8}{31r}$.

steps.title: Determine the slope of the other equation
steps.content: For no solution, the other line must be parallel to the given line, so it must share the same slope. The other equation therefore has slope $\frac{8}{31r}$. That is the correct answer.
